title: Feature Service Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: **AI Summary**: Every feature slice owns a colocated service (`{entity}.service.ts`) and models file (`{entity}.models.ts`). The service encapsulates all HTTP calls — CRUD, domain commands, lookups, sub-resources — and returns `Promise<T>` via `firstValueFrom()`. Components inject the service and use `async/await`; they never touch `HttpClient` directly. The models file defines plain TypeScript interfaces matching API contract shapes 1:1, using `EnumDTO` for all classification fields and `Nam...

examples.name: Each feature in modules/admin/{feature}/ contains
examples.code:
modules/admin/actions/
├── action.service.ts          # HTTP calls — owns the API surface
├── action.models.ts           # Interfaces — owns the data shapes
├── action-list.component.ts   # Grid page: injects ActionService
├── action-capture.component.ts # Form page: injects ActionService
└── ...

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: - Reference [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for global setup (`ngx-translate`, `ngx-toastr`, etc.)

sections.title: The Core Principle
content: > **Every feature slice owns its own service.** Components never use raw `HttpClient`.

The API puts queries and commands inside each feature folder — not in a global `Services/` directory. The web follows the same principle. A feature's HTTP service is the frontend equivalent of the API's query/command layer: it knows which endpoints to call, what request shapes to send, and what response shapes to expect. See [ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md).

sections.title: File Structure
content: ├── action.service.ts          # HTTP calls — owns the API surface
├── action.models.ts           # Interfaces — owns the data shapes

sections.title: Feature Service
content: Every feature service follows this structure:
- `environment.apiBaseUrl` for URL prefix (value: `'/api'` in all environments, BFF proxy pattern)
- Every method returns `Promise<T>` via `firstValueFrom()` — components use `async/await`
- Import shared types (`GridListResponse<T>`, `EnumDTO`, `NamedDTO`) from `@app/core/models/` — never redefine locally
- Import all feature type references from the colocated `{entity}.models.ts`
Feature services expose methods from these categories as needed.
// Save (upsert) — single POST handles both create and update
// Guid.Empty / empty string id = create, valid GUID id = update
Domain commands are named actions beyond CRUD — status changes, lifecycle transitions, priority changes. They operate on a specific entity by ID.
// No request body (action is the URL)
Operations on children of an aggregate root, accessed through the parent URL.
setEvidenceStatus(actionId: string, evidenceId: string, request: SetActionEvidenceStatusRequest): Promise<SetActionEvidenceStatusResponse> {
Lightweight endpoints that return dropdown data for use in other features.
// Entity lookups return NamedDTO when shape matches { id, name, description? }
Load unique values for client-side duplicate checking in capture pages. See [Validation — Duplicate Detection](validation.md).
When a feature needs data from another feature's API. The URL base differs from the service's own `apiBase`. Always use a typed return — never inline anonymous types.
| **Grid list** | `getGridList(page, pageSize, search?, ...filters?)` | `POST /grid-list` with body | List page loads grid data |
| **Flat list** | `list(filter?)` or `getList(filter?)` | `GET /` with params | Non-paged list (escalation rules, compliance profiles) |
| **Save (upsert)** | `save(request)` | `POST /` | All entities — single POST handles create and update |
| **Bulk delete** | `delete(request)` | `POST /delete` | All entities supporting bulk delete from list pages |
| **Domain command** | Descriptive verb: `updateStatus()`, `approve()`, `escalate()`, `revoke()` | `PUT` or `POST /{id}/{action}` | Lifecycle and state transitions |
| **Unique values** | `getUniqueNames(excludeId?)` | `GET /unique-names` | Duplicate detection in capture pages |
| **Cross-feature** | Feature-specific name | Varies | Reading from another feature's API |
| **`save(request)`** (upsert) | All entities — single POST handles create and update | Actions, Workflows, Compliance Profiles |

The upsert pattern matches the API's `SaveCommand` — a single POST handles both insert and update. The API determines the operation from the request's `id` field (`Guid.Empty` or empty string = insert, valid GUID = update).
| **`POST /delete` with `{ entityKeys: string[] }`** | All entities supporting bulk delete from list pages | Actions, Workflows |
All deletes use POST to `/delete` with a request body carrying entity keys.

sections.title: Feature Models
content: The models file defines plain TypeScript interfaces matching API contract shapes. All model types for a feature live in a single `{entity}.models.ts` file.

Classification fields and FK references use structured types that enable zero-transformation roundtrips between GET and Save. These shared types live in `core/models/` and MUST be imported — never redefined locally.
**EnumDTO** replaces bare strings for every enum/classification field. The API returns `{ id: 0, name: "Active" }` and the frontend sends the same shape back on Save — no mapping required:
// ✗ Wrong — bare string provides no type safety and requires manual mapping
status: string;               // what values are valid? nobody knows
// ✗ Wrong — union type requires frontend enum maps and transformation
status: ActionStatus;    // can't bind to dropdown without transformation
**NamedDTO** replaces bare GUIDs for FK references the UI displays by name:
workflow: NamedDTO;       // { id: "guid", name: "Safety Inspection", description: null }
complianceProfile: NamedDTO; // { id: "guid", name: "ISO 45001", description: null }
// ✗ Wrong — bare GUID requires a separate lookup to display the name
workflowId: string;        // what's the name? must call another endpoint
| API shape | Frontend type | Example fields |
| FK reference the UI displays by name | `NamedDTO` | `workflow`, `complianceProfile`, `escalationRule` |
The key benefit of EnumDTO/NamedDTO: zero frontend transformation ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)).
2. Frontend binds EnumDTO directly to dropdown
3. User selects "InProgress" → status is now { id: 1, name: "InProgress" }
Request sends the same EnumDTO shape back — no transformation
5. Backend reads status.Id, casts to enum, persists
Enum options for dropdowns come from lookup endpoints:
// → [{ id: 0, name: "Open" }, { id: 1, name: "InProgress" }, { id: 2, name: "Closed" }]
| **All models are plain interfaces** | No classes, no decorators, no inheritance |

| **EnumDTO for classification fields** | **MUST** use `EnumDTO` for any field that represents a status, priority, type, or enum on the backend ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)). Never bare `string` or union types. |
| **NamedDTO for FK references** | **MUST** use `NamedDTO` for any FK reference the UI displays by name ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)). Never bare GUIDs. |
| **Grid item DTOs are flat** | Counts instead of arrays (e.g., `evidenceCount` instead of `evidence[]`) |

| **ALL interfaces mirror API contract shapes** | Field names MUST match `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts` C# property names after camelCase conversion. If the backend has `EvidenceCount`, the frontend MUST have `evidenceCount` — not `evidencesCount`. Verify against the C# source, not assumptions. |
| **No transformation** | Frontend interfaces match API shapes 1:1 — EnumDTO/NamedDTO enable this |
| **Status fields are EnumDTO, never string** | Backend `Status` properties are always `EnumDTO` (with `JsonStringEnumConverter`). Frontend must use `status: EnumDTO`, never `status: string`. |
| **Nullable fields use `T \| null`** | Not optional (`?`) — matches API contract semantics |
| **Child DTOs colocated with parent** | `ActionEvidenceDTO` lives in `action.models.ts`, not a separate file |
| **One DTO per entity** | Don't create duplicate DTOs with different field names for the same entity |
| **Typed cross-feature references** | Define named interfaces for cross-feature data (e.g., `ComplianceProfileOption`), never inline anonymous types |
| **Import shared types** | `GridListResponse<T>`, `EnumDTO`, `NamedDTO` from `@app/core/models/` — never redefine locally |
All features define a typed delete request:

sections.title: Shared Types
content: Types used across multiple features live in `core/models/`:
| `GridListResponse<T>` | `core/models/grid-list-response.ts` | All grid services returning paged data |
| `NamedDTO` | `core/models/named-dto.ts` | All FK references displayed by name ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)) |

sections.title: Error Response Handling
content: The API returns RFC 7807 Problem Details for all errors. Define a shared parsing utility in `core/utils/`:
* Extract a user-facing error message from an API error response.
* Handles ProblemDetails (RFC 7807) and falls back to a default message.
export function parseApiError(err: unknown, fallback = 'An unexpected error occurred.'): string {
const httpErr = err as { error?: ProblemDetails };
* Extract the error code for programmatic handling (e.g., ACTION-002).
export function parseApiErrorCode(err: unknown): string | null {
const httpErr = err as { error?: ProblemDetails };
- MUST use `parseApiError()` for all API error handling — never inline ad-hoc parsing
- MUST pass a translated fallback message

sections.title: What Belongs Where
content: - All HTTP calls for the feature's API endpoints
- `firstValueFrom()` wrapping — components get Promises, not Observables
- Cross-feature API calls when this feature needs data from another resource's endpoints
- Enum lookup calls (`GET /api/v1/lookups/{enum-type}`) for dropdown options
- TypeScript interfaces matching API contract DTOs (using `EnumDTO` and `NamedDTO` for structured fields)
- Grid item interfaces (flat subset of full DTO)
- Request/response interfaces for the service methods
| Auth state (tokens, user info) | `core/services/auth.service.ts` | Used by every feature + interceptor |
| HTTP interceptors | `core/interceptors/` | Applied globally to all HTTP calls |
| Error handler | `core/services/` | Registered at app level |
| Reusable UI components | `shared/components/` | Used across admin |
| Grid/form utilities (`applyApiError`) | `shared/utils/` | Used by every edit component |
| `GridListResponse<T>`, `EnumDTO`, `NamedDTO` | `core/models/` | Used by all feature services and models |
### NOT in a Service (stays in the component)
| Form field signals | Component-local form state |
| Error display signals | Component-local validation state |
| API error parsing | Use shared `parseApiError()` from `core/utils/api-error.util.ts` |

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | **Service location** | Centralized in `core/services/` (`GridPageService`, `CapturePageService`) + feature `*-data.service.ts` | Colocated per feature (`{entity}.service.ts`) | [ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md) — mirrors API vertical slices |
| **DI style** | Constructor injection | `inject()` function | Modern Angular, no constructor parameters |
| **Observable handling** | `lastValueFrom()` | `firstValueFrom()` | Functionally identical for single-emission HTTP; `firstValueFrom` is the recommended RxJS function |

| **URL pattern** | `environment.apiUrl` + controller routes (`/Security/ApiKeys/GetGridList`) | `environment.apiBaseUrl` + RESTful routes (`/api/v1/actions`) | Actions API uses FastEndpoints with RESTful URL conventions |

| **Grid data loading** | POST-based `GetGridList` returning `{ gridRows: T[] }` | POST to `/grid-list` returning `GridListResponse<T>` | Actions API uses POST with filter payload returning generic grid response |
| **Delete pattern** | HTTP DELETE with body (`http.request('delete', ...)`) | POST to `/delete` with body | POST for body-carrying deletes avoids HTTP DELETE + body compatibility issues |

| **Caching** | `BaseCachedLookupService` with localStorage + TTL | No client caching — server handles it | Actions lookup endpoints are lightweight; caching adds complexity without benefit at current scale |
| **Model types** | Mix of classes (`NamedEntityModel`) and interfaces | All plain interfaces | Interfaces are sufficient for API shape matching; no runtime behavior needed |
| **Service naming** | `*DataService` | `*Service` | Shorter, no ambiguity in colocated context |

| **Enum/FK fields** | `NamedEntityModel` for FKs, no structured enum type | `EnumDTO` + `NamedDTO` matching Capstone's exact shapes | [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md) — cross-platform consistency, zero-transformation roundtrips |

- [List Page](list-page.md) — Grid page that consumes the service
- [Capture Page](capture-page.md) — Form page that consumes the service
- [Embedded List](embedded-list.md) — Child grids within capture pages
- [Capture State](capture-state.md) — State service for aggregate child routes
- [API Requests](../api/requests.md) — API-side contract patterns the models mirror

sections.title: Target Pattern (httpResource, ADR-0011)
content: > **Status: Proposed.** This section describes target patterns for feature services.
> The current patterns above remain in effect until migration is complete.
For read operations, `httpResource()` in the component replaces `firstValueFrom(http.get())` in the service. The service retains write methods (save, delete, domain commands).
// Write operations stay in the service
// Lookups stay if used by multiple components
body: { page: this.page(), pageSize: this.pageSize(), search: this.search() },
Frontend models split to match the backend's separate DTO and request types:
// Read shape (matches backend DTO with init setters)
// Write shape (matches backend SaveRequest with set setters)
id: string | null;            // null = create, string = update
**Key change:** The frontend no longer sends the full DTO back as the request. It constructs a `SaveRequest` with only writable fields. The `id` field is `string | null` (matching backend `Guid?`).
